title: Independent audit: license/consent/citation correctness (at least 95%)
context: The consent-first, provenance-tracked design of the corpus only holds if an independent reviewer verifies that the actual records match the claimed standards. Self-grading is not acceptable. This task assigns an auditor who is independent of the data contributors to check a stratified sample of the data-001 batch for consent validity, license correctness, and source/citation accuracy. The audit must find at least 95% of checked records correct; any consent, license, or citation defect blocks sign-off until fixed. Depends on place-name-pronunciation-data-001.
objective: Conduct an independent stratified audit of the data-001 batch (minimum 30 records or the whole batch if smaller) checking: consent record completeness and validity; license correctness of every source used; citation accuracy for IPA, respelling, and any provenance claims. Produce a written audit report with findings and a pass/fail verdict per check.
acceptanceCriteria: Auditor is independent of the contributors of the sampled records (no self-grading).
Sample size is at least 30 records or the whole batch if smaller than 30; stratified to cover both locales.
Each checked record is evaluated for: consent record completeness; license correctness of referenced sources; citation accuracy of provenance claims.
Overall correctness rate across all three checks is at least 95%.
Any consent, license, or citation defect found blocks sign-off and requires a fix-and-re-audit cycle.
Written audit report is committed (AUDIT-M1.md or equivalent) documenting sample selection, methodology, per-check results, and a final pass/fail verdict.
Privacy/consent reviewer signs off the audit report before the task is marked done.
output: AUDIT-M1.md audit report committed via PR, documenting the independent sample audit with at least 95% correctness verified and any defects resolved before sign-off.
